description: Lambda Overprovisioning Detection Rules — Identifies waste from over-allocated memory and dangerously high timeouts that amplify error costs

cost.note: Lambda duration billed in 1ms increments at $0.0000166667 per GB-second. Request cost $0.20 per 1M requests (first 1M free). Memory allocation directly scales duration cost.

severity_rules.name: memory_overprovisioning
severity_rules.condition: memory_used_mb_avg / memory_size_mb < memory_utilization_ratio_max, then refine scope with pricing_note: if pricing_note names a source->target memory tier (for example 3008MB -> 512MB), only functions on the named source tier are findings
severity_rules.description: 30-day average actual memory usage is below 50% of the allocated memory_size. Lambda bills for allocated memory, not used memory — over-allocation directly inflates duration costs. If cost_report pricing_note explicitly narrows the waste to a specific current tier and target tier, do not extrapolate that finding to functions already at the target tier or below.

severity_rules.name: timeout_misconfiguration
severity_rules.condition: timeout_sec >= timeout_reduce_threshold_sec AND duration_ms_avg < timeout_sec * 1000 * timeout_oversize_ratio_max
severity_rules.saving: parse from pricing_note in cost_report.json summary, or: error_rate * invocations_per_month * (timeout_sec - recommended_timeout_sec) * (memory_size_mb / 1024) * price_per_gb_second_usd
severity_rules.description: Function timeout is set far above actual p99 execution time. On error, Lambda charges for the full timeout duration. Reducing timeout to ~3× p99 cuts error-path billing by up to 99%.

memory_downsize_map.note: Recommended memory = next valid Lambda size above (max_memory_used_mb * headroom_multiplier). Use headroom_multiplier = 1.5. If pricing_note explicitly names a current tier and target tier for the scenario, treat that target as authoritative for matching functions.

timeout_recommendation.note: Recommended timeout = ceil(duration_ms_p99 / 1000) * 3, minimum 10 seconds. If pricing_note specifies a target, use that instead.
